query_title: The Internet's Own Boy: The Story of Aaron Swartz

recommendations.title: Why Did You Kill Me?
recommendations.overview: The line between justice and revenge blurs when a devastated family uses social media to track down the people who killed 24-year-old Crystal Theobald.

recommendations.title: What Happened, Miss Simone?
recommendations.overview: The film chronicles Nina Simone's journey from child piano prodigy to iconic musician and passionate activist, told in her own words.

recommendations.title: The Three Deaths of Marisela Escobedo
recommendations.overview: After the death of her daughter at the hand of her boyfriend, Marisela Escobedo began to fight for justice not only against the murderer but also against the corrupt Mexican judicial system.

recommendations.title: The Social Dilemma
recommendations.overview: This documentary-drama hybrid explores the dangerous human impact of social networking, with tech experts sounding the alarm on their own creations.

recommendations.title: The Cutting Edge: The Magic of Movie Editing
recommendations.overview: Documentary about the art of film editing. Clips are shown from many groundbreaking films with innovative editing styles.

recommendations.title: Jodorowsky's Dune

recommendations.title: Inside Asda: Bigger, Better, Cheaper?
recommendations.overview: The supermarket giant that rose high by taking prices low.

recommendations.title: RBG
recommendations.overview: Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg now 84, and still inspired by the lawyers who defended free speech during the Red Scare, Ginsburg refuses to relinquish her passionate duty, steadily fighting for equal rights for all citizens under the law. Through intima

recommendations.title: Pope Francis: A Man of His Word
recommendations.overview: Pope Francis responds to questions from around the world, discussing topics including ecology, immigration, consumerism and social justice.

recommendations.title: The Edge of Democracy
recommendations.overview: A cautionary tale for these times of democracy in crisis—the personal and political fuse to explore one of the most dramatic periods in Brazilian history. With unprecedented access to Presidents Dilma Rousseff and Lula da Silva, we witness their rise
